population: Patients with abdominal sepsis requiring open abdomen (OA) as part of Damage Control Surgery
notes: 113 of 1474 patients operated for abdominal sepsis (7.6%) underwent OA. Overall mortality was 43.4%, morbidity 76.6%, DFC rate 97.8%.

population: Patients with open abdomen (OA) treatment for abdominal sepsis/emergency (bowel perforation, bowel infarction, abdominal abscesses, infected necrotizing pancreatitis)
predictor: Overall mortality during OA treatment and within 30 days of definitive fascial closure
timing: During OA treatment and within 30 days from definitive fascial closure
method: Descriptive statistics, chi-square or Fisher's exact test (p-value reported)
effect_size: 20 patients (40.8%) deceased during OA treatment; 29 (59.2%) deceased within 30 days of fascial closure
performance: Overall mortality 43.4%; p=0.02 for timing of death comparison
notes: Causes of death: cardiopulmonary complications (55%), multiorgan failure due to sepsis (41%), irreversible brain damage (4%). No mortality between 30 and 90 days.

population: Patients with open abdomen (OA) treatment for abdominal sepsis/emergency
notes: None

population: OA surviving patients
notes: 10 patients (10.8%) required a prosthetic mesh; skin was contextually closed in 94.6% of cases.

population: Patients with open abdomen (OA) treatment for abdominal sepsis/emergency

notes: None

population: Patients with open abdomen (OA) treatment for abdominal sepsis/emergency
predictor: OA duration and number of looks
effect_size: Mean OA duration: 2.8 ± 1.7 days; Mean number of looks: 1.2 ± 0.8
notes: 49% of cases presented a 1A grade on modified Björck classification at 2nd look.

population: Septic patients undergoing open abdomen (OA) for septic shock due to abdominal diseases
notes: ASA score IV identified as a predictive factor for mortality in patients undergoing OA for septic shock due to abdominal diseases. 88.3% of patients had ASA score greater than II. Definitive fascial closure obtained within 3 days in 97.8% of patients; only 25% required more than two surgical revisions.

population: Patients undergoing open abdomen (OA) management with temporary abdominal closure (TAC)
predictor: BMI, ASA score, APACHE II score, TAC/NPWT technique, OA duration, ICU stay
notes: Bold p-values rounded to second decimal reported for significant findings. Analysis of ASA carried out by two levels: IV vs III or less. BMI analyzed as 30 or over vs 29 or less. One variable analyzed as 1A vs 1B or over. Heterogeneity and methodology bias noted among included studies. Negative pressure wound therapy associated with greater efficacy for definitive fascial closure.

population: Septic patients treated with open abdomen (OA)
outcome: Not explicitly named outcome (binary logistic regression dependent variable, likely mortality or failure of definitive fascial closure)
method: Multivariate binary logistic regression with stepwise backward selection
notes: Frailty clinical scale was significant in both Step 1 and Step 5 of the multivariate model. The direction of the OR changed between steps, which may reflect model refinement during stepwise selection.

population: Patients undergoing open abdomen (OA) with NPWT for various indications including abdominal sepsis
predictor: Timing of death relative to fascial closure (during OA vs. after definitive fascial closure)
timing: During OA vs. after definitive fascial closure
effect_size: 40.8% mortality during OA vs. 59.2% after definitive fascial closure
notes: No differences in OA duration, number of looks, or Björck classification were found between survivors and non-survivors

population: Patients undergoing open abdomen (OA) with NPWT
predictor: OA duration, number of looks, Björck classification
notes: These variables did not differ between survivors and non-survivors, suggesting mortality was not solely a direct consequence of ongoing abdominal sepsis

population: Patients undergoing open abdomen (OA) with NPWT
effect_size: EAF rate 2.2% in current series vs. up to 26% in literature
notes: Low EAF rate speculated to be related to relatively short OA duration of 2.8 days
